name: incursa/workbench metadata catalog

grouping_key_registry.name: Standard
grouping_key_registry.description: Core definitions, traceability model, and publication rules

grouping_key_registry.name: Templates
grouping_key_registry.description: Document templates and compact requirement grammar

notes: specification identifiers use the SPEC-{domain}{grouping} template and stop after the optional grouping segments
grouping expands to zero or more '-SEGMENT' tokens after the domain token
grouping segments must start with a letter, so numeric-only grouping values are invalid
the terminal sequence is zero-padded to at least four digits
domain and grouping segments use the same uppercase alphanumeric token pattern
the reference package allows zero or more grouping segments and does not set a hard maximum
grouping-key registry entries provide the human-readable names and descriptions for the abbreviated codes used in the spec suite
requirement identifiers use the same domain and grouping rules as specification identifiers but use the REQ prefix and terminal sequence because they identify clauses within specifications
tests and code may reference requirement identifiers directly
